scope: The spec query full-text retrieval command. Belongs here: FTS5 MATCH semantics; rank-ascending results; superseded exclusion; --limit bounds. Does not belong here: file→requirement resolution (→ RSLV). Boundary case: a bare AND OR FTS5 grammar error is a QURY usage-error (exit 2) contract.

requirements.statement: spec query answers full-text retrieval over requirement text and rationale via FTS5.
requirements.why: Rung-1 agent retrieval — a semantic-ish hit a plain grep for the id would miss.

requirements.statement: spec query matches porter-stemmed forms of the search text, so querying 'renewal charge' returns a requirement whose text says 'renews'.
requirements.why: Without stemming, agents must guess the author's exact wording; the headline find-the-requirement-by-the-value-moment retrieval promise silently fails.

requirements.statement: spec query returns TERM-domain glossary definitions in a Terms group beside requirement hits, discriminated by the FtsHit key so a term hit is distinguishable from a requirement hit, while terms stay excluded from the coverage matrix
requirements.why: the glossary becomes searchable at query time — the only-allowed-jargon payoff of §4.11 is visible where an author is already looking, without leaking terms into coverage obligations
